verses.text: The destroyer comes up against Nineveh; let her keep watch, for God will restore 'the excellency of Jacob.' Tables turned. The city that ruined others now faces her own siege.
The destroyer comes up against Nineveh; let her keep watch, for God will restore 'the excellency of Jacob.' Tables turned. The city that ruined others now faces her own siege.
The attackers' shields are red, the chariots rage in the streets; her defenders stumble in their haste. Furious assault. The proud city is thrown into panic.
The attackers' shields are red, the chariots rage in the streets; her defenders stumble in their haste. Furious assault. The proud city is thrown into panic.
The attackers' shields are red, the chariots rage in the streets; her defenders stumble in their haste. Furious assault. The proud city is thrown into panic.
The river gates open, and Nineveh, 'like a pool of water,' drains away; 'Stand, stand,' they cry, but none looks back. Fleeing in vain. The mighty city empties before her foe.
The river gates open, and Nineveh, 'like a pool of water,' drains away; 'Stand, stand,' they cry, but none looks back. Fleeing in vain. The mighty city empties before her foe.
The river gates open, and Nineveh, 'like a pool of water,' drains away; 'Stand, stand,' they cry, but none looks back. Fleeing in vain. The mighty city empties before her foe.
Take the spoil of silver and gold; she is left 'empty, and void, and waste,' hearts melting, knees smiting. Stripped and terrified. The hoarded wealth of cruelty is plundered.
Take the spoil of silver and gold; she is left 'empty, and void, and waste,' hearts melting, knees smiting. Stripped and terrified. The hoarded wealth of cruelty is plundered.
Where now is 'the dwelling of the lions'? 'Behold, I am against thee, saith the LORD'; He will burn her chariots and end her prey. God the adversary. The hunter of nations is hunted by God.
